rankedFormat.battleStyle: Doubles (VGC-style): bring 6, field 4 (2+2 on the field)
rankedFormat.teamPreview: ~90 seconds: you see species and TYPES of the opposing 6 (the type icons are the reliable information for identification)
rankedFormat.rankLadder: Ball ladder (…Ultra Ball → Master Ball = top tier, then an internal numeric rating). Below Master Ball you win off opponents' mistakes; within Master Ball, teams and margins decide.

statSystem.statPoints: "EVs" are called Stat Points: 66 total points, max 32 per stat (typical offensive spread: 32/32/2; bulk: 32HP/32Def)
statSystem.statAlignment: Equivalent to natures: +10% to one stat, −10% to another
statSystem.retraining: Everything can be re-allocated with training tickets (no grinding/breeding)
statSystem.warning: Replica codes also transfer the spreads, but ALWAYS verify the stats screen after replicating (some unexpected spreads are deliberate builds, e.g. bulky Zard-Y 28HP/14Def/23Spe)

rules: Only 1 Mega per battle; the stone is an ITEM (occupies the item slot)
You can bring MORE than one stone among your 6 (double Mega option, you choose in battle which one to evolve)
The pre-Mega ability applies until you evolve (e.g. Gengar = Cursed Body, as Mega = Shadow Tag: the trapping kicks in ONLY after the Mega)
megaEvolution.championsExclusives: Champions INVENTS Megas that don't exist in the main games, with NEW ABILITIES. Seen in the meta so far: Mega Floette, Mega Scovillain, Mega Glimmora, Mega Delphox, Mega Golurk, Mega Meganium, Mega Starmie, Mega Skarmory (ability: STALWART — ignores redirection!), Mega Dragonite, Mega Froslass. Full new-Mega list per Serebii also includes: Victreebel, Clefable, Feraligatr, Chimecho, Emboar, Excadrill, Audino, Chandelure, Chesnaught, Greninja, Meowstic, Hawlucha, Crabominable, Drampa — plus Mega Raichu arriving with Reg M-B (17/06). Roster flags: see roster.json megaAvailable.
megaEvolution.debugRule: When an interaction looks impossible under the classic rules → ask yourself "was it Mega? what's its ability in Champions?"
megaEvolution.cost: Mega Stone = 2,000 VP at the Frontier Shop; some free from tutorial/Battle Pass

itemSystem.itemClause: Each item at most ONCE per team (unlike mainline VGC)
itemSystem.poolSize: ~30 non-berry items at launch + berries + type boosters + Mega Stones
itemSystem.movepoolNote: Movepools differ from mainline: e.g. Incineroar WITHOUT Knock Off and U-turn in M-A

economy.vp: Victory Points: the main currency, earned by battling. Mega Stone 2,000 VP; replica/training per Pokémon ~95–1,500 VP depending on how far it is from the target set
economy.recruitRanch: Pokémon recruitment with tickets, RNG outcome (you don't choose who comes out) → missing pieces are long-term goals, not purchases
economy.trainingTickets: Change moves/stats/abilities of Pokémon you own
economy.replicaTeams: Enter a Team ID (10 characters) → you see the full preview and copy the SETS onto Pokémon YOU OWN, paying per mon. Codes die if the creator edits/deletes the team.

fieldMechanics.weatherWar: The LAST setter to enter wins (abilities trigger on entry → re-switching your setter re-sets the weather)
fieldMechanics.sun: Fire +50% (for EVERYONE), Water −50% (for EVERYONE), Solar Beam without charging
fieldMechanics.rain: Water +50%, Fire −50%, Hurricane/Thunder 100%, Electro Shot without charging
fieldMechanics.sand: Chip damage + Sand Rush Speed ×2 + Rock-types' SpD +50%
fieldMechanics.snow: Aurora Veil enabled, Ice-types' Def +50%, Blizzard 100%
fieldMechanics.psychicTerrain: Blocks ALL priority moves against grounded targets (Fake Out, Sucker Punch, Jet Punch, Extreme Speed, Prankster…) + boosts Psychic
fieldMechanics.trickRoom: 5 turns, reverses the order WITHIN each priority bracket (priority moves still go first)

gotchas.title: Fake Out
gotchas.text: Works ONLY on the turn of entry; it's Normal → 0 on Ghosts.

gotchas.title: Armor Tail
gotchas.text: Blocks PRIORITY moves aimed at Farigiraf or its ally (Fake Out, Sucker, Prankster Taunt…). Does NOT block normal moves or sleep.

gotchas.title: Taunt vs Encore
gotchas.text: Taunt only blocks status moves (a Taunted mon can still attack). Encore locks in the last move used for 3 turns — applied BEFORE the target acts, it OVERRIDES the move it picked that turn (the faster mon gets its Encore off first). Switching cures Encore; a mon that just switched in can't be Encored.

gotchas.title: Powders
gotchas.text: Spore, Sleep Powder, Rage Powder…: GRASS-types and Overcoat are IMMUNE.

gotchas.title: Rage Powder / Follow Me
gotchas.text: Last ONE turn; only redirect single-target moves; the redirect dies if the redirector switches out/faints mid-turn; do NOT redirect spread/sound moves; bypassed by Stalwart (Mega Skarmory, Archaludon) and Propeller Tail (Barraskewda).

gotchas.title: Trapping (Shadow Tag)
gotchas.text: GHOSTS are immune; pivot moves (U-turn/Volt Switch/Flip Turn/Parting Shot/Teleport) still let you leave; whoever is caged at move selection stays caged that turn.

gotchas.title: Perish Song
gotchas.text: Counter of 3 on EVERYONE present when it's sung (including the singer); −1 at end of turn; KO at 0; switching resets the counter; Soundproof is immune; Magic Bounce REFLECTS it; Good as Gold is immune; singing again does NOT refresh existing counters; ignores boosts, Sash, screens, Unaware, Magic Guard.

gotchas.title: Throat Chop
gotchas.text: The target can't use SOUND moves for 2 turns (Perish Song, Hyper Voice, Clanging Scales, Clangorous Soul); switching clears the silence.

gotchas.title: Quash
gotchas.text: The target acts LAST that turn ("soft" anti-redirect/anti-priority).

gotchas.title: Instruct
gotchas.text: Oranguru: the partner immediately repeats its last move = 2 attacks/turn.

gotchas.title: Sucker Punch
gotchas.text: Fails if the target isn't using an attacking move (Protect/status/switch).

gotchas.title: Scrappy
gotchas.text: Lopunny-M, Kangaskhan: their Normal/Fighting moves HIT Ghosts.

gotchas.title: Normals immune to Ghost
gotchas.text: Shadow Ball = 0 on Lopunny/Kangaskhan/Ursaluna/Snorlax/Maushold and on the part-Normals (Farigiraf, Indeedee) — Ghost attackers struggle.

gotchas.title: Thermal Exchange
gotchas.text: Baxcalibur: Fire hits give it +1 Attack and it can't be burned — anti-sun tech.

gotchas.title: Defiant / Competitive
gotchas.text: Competitive (Milotic): +2 SpA when its stats drop → Intimidate is a GIFT. Defiant (Kingambit): +2 Atk, same story.

gotchas.title: Unburden
gotchas.text: Speed ×2 when the item is consumed (White Herb after Close Combat = auto-combo); paralysis negates it all.

gotchas.title: Last Respects / Supreme Overlord
gotchas.text: Last Respects: 50 + 50 × fallen allies. Supreme Overlord: +20% Atk per fallen ally. "Lots of fast KOs" plans (Perish included) CHARGE both of these.

gotchas.title: Moves with constraints
gotchas.text: Gigaton Hammer: can't be used twice in a row. Solar Beam: never without sun (charges; also halved in rain). Electro Shot: charges without rain.

gotchas.title: Illusion / Disguise
gotchas.text: Zoroark enters looking like the last mon in the party; revealed on first damage; spread moves unmask it. Mimikyu (Disguise): negates the first hit.

gotchas.title: Stance Change
gotchas.text: Aegislash: Shield form until it attacks; King's Shield to return to Shield; Poltergeist fails if the target holds no item.

gotchas.title: Wide Guard
gotchas.text: Stops spread moves (Heat Wave/Rock Slide/EQ/Hyper Voice…), not single-target ones. In the Italian UI it appears as "Bodyguard".

gotchas.title: Switching cures
gotchas.text: Encore, Taunt, Throat Chop silence, perish count, stat drops, Disable.

gotchas.title: Opposing Intimidate-cycling
gotchas.text: It exists against you too: every Incineroar re-entry = −1 to your physical attackers.

localization.note: IT/EN move/item names differ: a mapping table is needed if the app reads Italian screenshots. To be expanded.

assets.licenseWarning: For a PUBLIC/monetized app the Showdown sprite license is a gray area: consider custom icons or no images at all. The scan database will store only HASHES (numbers), never the assets.
